context.summary: Create a wireless intruder alarm that will warn you when someone steps on a home-made pressure sensor.
how_it_works: Flash the sensor program onto a micro:bit.

Make a pressure input switch out of cardboard and tin foil like in the picture. Fold it over and place it under a rug or carpet. You may need to add some foam to keep each side apart. Connect the two foil pads on one side to pins 0 and GND on the sensor micro:bit. When you step on it, the foil on the top completes an electrical circuit, and it sends an ГўВҖВҳintruderГўВҖВҷ radio message.

Flash the alarm program on to the other micro:bit (the alarm) and attach a buzzer or speaker to pin 0 and GND if you have one. When it receives the ГўВҖВҳintruderГўВҖВҷ message it shows an angry face on the LED display and plays an audible alarm sound. Press button A to clear the display.
improve_it: Add multiple sensors each sending its own message, e.g. ГўВҖВҳroom1ГўВҖВҷ, ГўВҖВҳroom2ГўВҖВҷ and have the alarm show where the intruder is.
Try different designs for the pressure switch. For example, place a foam pad inside it if is being triggered too easily.
You could make a rain alarm using two tin foil pads very close together ГўВҖВ“ if they get wet, they will trigger the alarm. Be sure not to get your micro:bit wet though!
